We begin with the phrase 'the autopilot shall change states from NOMINAL to MANEUVER', which indicates a transition condition between two states of the autopilot. In LTL, a transition implies that certain conditions must not hold at the same time. The transition occurs when 'the sensor data is not good', which can be interpreted in terms of the 'good' atomic proposition, in this case, as 'not good' being the negation of good, or !good. Therefore, the condition for changing from NOMINAL to MANEUVER is that while the autopilot is in NOMINAL (state_is_NOMINAL holds), if the sensor data is not good (!good), then the autopilot will transition to MANEUVER (state_is_MANEUVER holds). The transition must be formulated with a temporal operator which is logical to imply it happens in the next state, thus producing the expression 'state_is_NOMINAL & !good -> X state_is_MANEUVER' for the immediate next state. Therefore, the final LTL formula is: G(state_is_NOMINAL & !good -> X state_is_MANEUVER).
